rationale: In the literature review, the citing paper gives Naik and Raman (2003) a distinct role by summarizing its dynamic multimedia allocation result rather than just listing it. The authors then explicitly argue that this prior work ignores spatial proximity and neighborhood effects, which motivates their own research gap. They frame their contribution as adding those missing spatial elements to normative advertising allocation, so the citation materially supports positioning and conceptual setup.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Naik and Raman (2003) as prior dynamic normative advertising-allocation research and extends that line to a spatiotemporal setting. It adds spatial heterogeneity, neighborhood effects, and spatial/serial dependence to derive national-regional budget allocation rules.
